description: Raw data downloaded from GEO (GSE213216). 54 samples in tar.gz format. Total size ~15.7GB.
Per-sample QC: filtered cells with ≥500 genes and ≤20% mitochondrial reads. All genes retained. 43 samples successfully processed (2 failed due to missing raw matrix).
Combined all 43 filtered samples into a single AnnData object. Total cells: ~283,000. Genes: 33,538. Added sample metadata column 'tissue_type' (initially unknown).

scVI-VAE trained on combined AnnData (epoch 400, ELBO: -1842.3). Latent dim d=20, batch_key = 'sample'. Denoises raw counts and computes latent representations for downstream analysis. Model saved with paired h5ad file (v0.4.0).

Final QC applied to combined data: cells with ≥1000 total counts, ≤20% mitochondrial reads, ≤8000 genes detected (to exclude multiplets). Retained 247,990 cells and 38,224 genes. scVI latent representation computed (X_scVI in .obsm), UMAP generated from latent space. No clustering applied. Raw counts preserved for downstream marker binarisation.

Surface marker lists generated by intersecting all genes in v0.4.0 with the Surfaceome database (Bausch-Fluck et al. 2018; ~3000 validated human cell-surface proteins). Outputs: full metadata (surfaceome_in_atlas.csv), CSPA-validated shortlist (surfaceome_in_atlas_CSPA_validated.csv), and plain gene list (marker_shortlist.txt). Used for subsetting before denoised expression extraction.

Enhanced h5ad with cell-type annotations (CellTypist + Human Endometrium Atlas) and full patient metadata scraped from GEO. Immune cells flagged and stratified separately. Built on v0.4.0 as foundation. Ready for marker specificity scoring and cross-cell-type analysis.

Binarised cell-level data ready for differentiable optimisation. Derived from v0.6.0 by subsetting to surface markers and binarising posterior scVI expression estimates (threshold >0.5 per 10k library normalisation). Stored as sparse numpy arrays. Two versions provided: with and without patient IDs for stratified sampling during training.

Differentiable optimisation engine results. Reparameterised combinatorial search (4.5M marker pairs) into continuous space using Gumbel-softmax relaxation. Optimisation performed patient-stratified and immune-filtered. Top hit: GAS1 + BST2 with ~10x enrichment in lesion cells (test split). Consistent top attractor across 20 random seeds. FAP (fibroblast activation protein) also highly enriched, aligning with Aghajanian et al. 2019 fibroblast-targeting CAR strategy. Ready for cross-species safety validation.

Cross-species safety validation (Tabula Muris atlas). GAS1 + BST2 dual-target pair screened against mouse homologs to assess off-target expression in heart, lung, liver, and kidney. Preliminary results support safety profile (minimal cardiac/pulmonary expression). Full Tabula Sapiens validation deferred due to computational constraints (50GB+ dataset and 38k genes would require distributed scVI training). Plan: complete validation via HPC cluster access or institutional compute allocation.

Preprint draft finalised and submitted to bioRxiv. Title: 'Computationally-Guided Identification of Lesion-Specific Dual-Target CAR-T Markers in Endometriosis via Differentiable Optimisation'. Authors: Haziq (IfWH, UCL), TS (MIT), Abdurrahman (pre-university), and collaborators. Describes complete pipeline: scRNA-seq integration → surface marker subsetting → patient-stratified specificity scoring → differentiable optimisation of marker pairs → cross-species validation. Currently under review.
